title: 10 best herbal tinctures for tendinitis
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for tendinitis are concentrated liquid extracts of medicinal herbs, often taken orally to alleviate tendinitis symptoms. These tinctures may include anti-inflammatory and analgesic herbs like turmeric, willow bark, and arnica. Turmeric, for example, contains curcumin, which inhibits inflammatory enzymes, reducing pain and swelling. Willow bark, on the other hand, has salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, that helps relieve pain. Arnica, rich in helenalin, is known to reduce inflammation and improve mobility. By incorporating these herbal tinctures into their treatment plans, individuals with tendinitis can experience reduced pain, increased flexibility, and improved overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower tincture helps with tendinitis because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. Arnica has been used for centuries to treat various types of inflammation and injuries, including tendinitis. The tincture is made by soaking arnica flowers in alcohol, which extracts the active compounds. When applied topically, it can increase blood flow to the area, promoting healing and reducing pain. Arnica's anti-inflammatory properties work by inhibiting the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, providing relief to individuals suffering from tendinitis. Its soothing and calming effects make it a popular natural remedy for those seeking a safe and effective alternative to over-the-counter or prescription medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica flower tincture helps reduce inflammation, a key factor in tendinitis, due to its active constituents such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids.

Analgesic: Arnica flower tincture alleviates pain caused by tendinitis by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are involved in pain signaling, thanks to its helenalin and dihydrohelenalin content.

Improves circulation: Arnica flower tincture enhances blood flow, promoting healing and reducing swelling in tendinitis, as a result of increased blood vessel dilation from its essential oils and resins.
Antioxidant: Arnica flower tincture neutralizes free radicals, reducing cellular damage and promoting tissue repair in tendinitis, owing to its high flavonoid and phenolic acid content.

Immune system support: Arnica flower tincture stimulates the immune system, aiding in the body's natural response to injury and inflammation in tendinitis, by activating white blood cells with its sesquiterpene lactones and saponins.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The arnica flower contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it useful for tendinitis.
Leaves: The leaves of the arnica plant also contain these beneficial compounds, and can be used in making medicinal tinctures for tendinitis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the arnica plant, which are similar to roots, contain high levels of therapeutic compounds, and are commonly used in making tinctures for tendinitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arnica montana flowers at dawn or mid-morning when they are fully open and free of moisture.
Cleanly dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at low temperature for 6 hours.
Chop the dried flowers into small pieces to increase their surface area and facilitate extraction with vodka.
Combine one part chopped arnica montana flowers with three parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and stir gently for 5 minutes.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and transferring to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Daisy tincture helps with tendinitis because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate the pain and swelling associated with the condition. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, work together to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected tendons. Additionally, daisy tincture has been shown to improve circulation, which can help speed up the recovery process and reduce stiffness in the affected area. Its natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties make daisy tincture a popular alternative remedy for tendinitis, providing relief and support for those suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Daisy tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in tendinitis, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Analgesic: The analgesic effect of daisy tincture alleviates tendinitis-related pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are involved in pain signaling, due to the active constituent, arnicin.

Antispasmodic: Daisy tincture's antispasmodic properties help relax muscles and alleviate spasms, providing relief for tendinitis-related muscle tension, attributed to the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids.

Circulatory stimulant: The circulatory stimulant effect of daisy tincture promotes blood flow to the affected area, aiding in the healing process of tendinitis, due to the active constituent, helenalin.

Immune-boosting: Daisy tincture's immune-boosting properties help the body fight off infection and promote overall healing, which is crucial for tendinitis recovery, due to the presence of various polysaccharides and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Daisy roots contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce tendon swelling and alleviate pain associated with tendinitis.
Leaves: The leaves of the daisy plant have been used in tinctures to help soothe irritated tendons and promote healing in individuals with tendinitis.
Flowers: Daisy flowers are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that can help reduce inflammation and provide pain relief for those suffering from tendinitis.
Seeds: Daisy seeds contain essential oils and other compounds that can help improve circulation and reduce inflammation, making them a valuable addition to tinctures for tendinitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bellis perennis flowers and leaves at dawn or evening when potency is highest.
Wash the harvested plant material with clean water to remove dirt and debris gently.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces and combine 50g of it with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a glass jar tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure proper extraction thoroughly.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container and discard the solids immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with tendinitis because it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate the pain and inflammation associated with the condition. The herb contains flavonoids, such as lutein and zeaxanthin, that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair. Additionally, pot marigold tincture can improve blood flow to the affected area, which can help speed up the healing process. The tincture can be taken orally or applied topically to the affected area for maximum benefit. Overall, pot marigold tincture is a natural and effective way to manage tendinitis symptoms and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation in tendinitis by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins and leukotrienes, thanks to its active constituents, such as flavonoids and triterpene alcohols.
Analgesic: The tincture provides pain relief by desensitizing nerve endings and reducing pain perception, due to its active components like salicylate and volatile oils.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tincture helps prevent infection in tendinitis injuries by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi, attributed to its active constituents, such as thiophenes and flavonoids.

Circulatory stimulant: The tincture improves blood flow and nutrient delivery to injured tendons, promoting healing and reducing inflammation, because of its active components, such as volatile oils and saponins.

Tissue repair promotion: Pot marigold tincture supports tendon repair by stimulating the production of collagen and elastin fibers, thanks to its active constituents, such as flavonoids and triterpenes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are used in medicinal tinctures for tendinitis due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant are often included in tinctures for tendinitis, as they contain high levels of flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory effects.

Stems: Although less commonly used, the stems of the pot marigold plant can also be included in tinctures for tendinitis, as they contain beneficial compounds that may help reduce inflammation and promote healing.

Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are rich in alkaloids and other compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making them a useful addition to medicinal tinctures for tendinitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and rinse them gently with clean water.
Chop the dried or fresh flowers into smaller pieces using scissors or a mortar and pestle.
Combine 100g of chopped flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the solids and transferring the liquid to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with tendinitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate the pain and inflammation associated with the condition. The herb's active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, reducing swelling and tenderness in the affected area. Additionally, St. John's wort may help improve blood flow and promote tissue repair, which can accelerate the healing process and restore mobility to the affected tendon. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, St. John's wort tincture can provide effective relief from tendinitis symptoms and support overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture helps reduce inflammation in tendinitis due to the presence of active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin.
Analgesic: It alleviates pain associated with tendinitis by inhibiting the reuptake of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and noradrenaline.
Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic property of St. John's wort helps relax muscles and alleviate spasms, which can provide relief in tendinitis.
Antimicrobial: This property helps prevent infections in tendinitis injuries, as active compounds like hypericin and hyperforin have antimicrobial properties.
Circulation-enhancing: By improving blood flow, St. John's wort tincture aids in delivering essential nutrients and oxygen to the affected area, promoting healing in tendinitis.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain high levels of hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for tendinitis.
Leaves: Rich in active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin, that help reduce pain and inflammation in tendinitis.
Stems: Contain medicinal constituents, although to a lesser extent than flowers and leaves, still contributing to the anti-inflammatory effects of tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum flowers at peak bloom and dry them immediately in a warm place for 2-4 weeks.
Grind the dried st john's wort flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or food processor.
Combine 100 grams of the powdered st john's wort with 700 milliliters of 60% to 70% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 weeks, shaking the container daily, then strain it through cheesecloth into another container.
Transfer the tincture to dark-glass bottles and store them in a cool place for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tincture helps with tendinitis because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce pain and inflammation in the affected tendons. Comfrey is rich in allantoin, a substance that promotes cell proliferation and healing, making it effective in accelerating the recovery process. Additionally, comfrey tincture can improve joint mobility and reduce stiffness, allowing for improved range of motion and function. Its ability to stimulate the production of new tissue and promote the healing of damaged tendons makes comfrey tincture a valuable natural remedy for tendinitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of allantoin, rosmarinic acid, and tannins, helps reduce inflammation and swelling in tendons, providing relief from tendinitis pain.
Analgesic: The analgesic effect of comfrey tincture, attributed to its rosmarinic acid content, aids in alleviating tendinitis-related pain by inhibiting pain-signaling molecules.
Tissue regeneration: Comfrey tincture's ability to promote tissue regeneration, thanks to its allantoin and rosmarinic acid content, helps heal damaged tendons, supporting tendinitis recovery.

Improved circulation: Comfrey tincture enhances blood flow to the affected area, facilitating faster healing and pain reduction, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid, which has a vasodilatory effect.

Antioxidant: Comfrey tincture's antioxidant properties, attributed to its rosmarinic acid content, help protect tendon tissues from oxidative damage, reducing the risk of further inflammation and tendinitis progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots contain high amounts of allantoin, a substance that helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing of tendon injuries.
Leaves: Comfrey leaves are rich in rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for making medicinal tinctures for tendinitis.
Flowers: Comfrey flowers contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to alleviate pain and inflammation associated with tendinitis.
Stems: Comfrey stems contain mucilage, which has a soothing effect on irritated tissues, and can help to reduce pain and inflammation in tendinitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh comfrey leaves and flowers in their prime, ensuring a minimum of one pound per batch.
Chop the comfrey material into small pieces to facilitate infusion, using scissors or a knife.
Combine the chopped comfrey with 80-100 proof vodka or grain alcohol at a ratio of one part herb to three parts solvent.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily to ensure thorough extraction and tincture formation.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and labeling the finished tincture with date and dosage information.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew tincture helps with tendinitis because it contains parthenolide, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. Tendinitis is an inflammation of the tendons, the fibrous tissues that attach muscles to bones. By reducing inflammation, feverfew tincture can alleviate the pain and swelling associated with tendinitis. Additionally, feverfew has been found to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, further helping to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects make feverfew tincture a valuable herbal remedy for individuals suffering from tendinitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Feverfew tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce tendon swelling and pain in tendinitis, due to the presence of parthenolide.
Analgesic: Feverfew tincture's analgesic effects alleviate tendinitis-related pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, thanks to parthenolide.

Antispasmodic: Feverfew tincture's antispasmodic action relaxes muscles and reduces muscle spasms, providing relief for individuals with tendinitis, attributed to parthenolide and other sesquiterpene lactones.
Circulation-enhancing: Feverfew tincture improves blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing inflammation, due to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils.

Immune-modulating: Feverfew tincture helps regulate the immune response, preventing excessive inflammation and promoting tissue repair in tendinitis, attributed to parthenolide and other active constituents.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the feverfew plant are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for tendinitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the feverfew plant are used in making medicinal tinctures for tendinitis because of their pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects.
Seeds: The seeds of the feverfew plant are used in making medicinal tinctures for tendinitis due to their ability to reduce inflammation and provide pain relief.
Stems: The stems of the feverfew plant are used in making medicinal tinctures for tendinitis because of their anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean the feverfew leaves, stems and flowers, ensuring 100g of dried plant material.
Combine the dried feverfew with 70% vodka in a glass jar, filling it almost to the top with a ratio of 1:3.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking it daily for at least 5 minutes.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, pressing on solids to extract liquid.
Label and store the final tincture in a cool dark place, using within 2 years of making.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow tincture helps with tendinitis because it contains salicin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties similar to aspirin. Tendinitis is an inflammation of the tendons, which can cause pain and swelling. The salicin in white willow tincture can help reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating the pain and promoting healing of the tendons. Additionally, white willow tincture has been used traditionally for its pain-relieving effects, making it a popular natural remedy for tendinitis. However, it is important to note that white willow tincture may take longer to provide relief than over-the-counter pain medications, and it may not be suitable for everyone, especially those with allergies to aspirin or other salicylates.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White willow tincture contains salicin, which is a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce swelling and pain in tendinitis.
Analgesic: The salicin in white willow tincture is converted to salicylic acid in the body, acting as a pain reliever that alleviates tenderness and discomfort in tendinitis.
Fever-reducing: White willow tincture's salicin also helps lower fever, which can be a symptom of tendinitis, allowing the body to focus on healing.
Improves blood flow: White willow tincture contains flavonoids, which are antioxidants that improve blood flow and help reduce inflammation in tendinitis.
Reduces muscle spasms: White willow tincture has tannins, which have an astringent effect that can help reduce muscle spasms and relax the affected area in tendinitis.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the white willow plant is commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for tendinitis due to its high salicin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the white willow plant are used in making medicinal tinctures for tendinitis because they contain salicin, which helps relieve pain and reduce inflammation.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the white willow plant are used in making medicinal tinctures for tendinitis due to their high concentration of salicin, which has pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salix alba leaves at peak growth stage during full moon phase.
Cleanse the harvested leaves by rinsing with cold water and air-drying for 24 hours.
Chop 100g of dried or fresh leaves into small pieces using sharp scissors or a grinder.
Combine chopped leaves with 50% vodka (350ml) in a clean glass jar, steeping for 2-6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another jar, discarding solids and storing tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with tendinitis because it has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The herb, Achillea millefolium, contains compounds like sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected tendons. This can provide relief from the pain and discomfort associated with tendinitis. Additionally, yarrow has been shown to improve blood circulation, which can promote healing and tissue repair in the affected area. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, yarrow tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of tendinitis and support overall tendon health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, helps reduce inflammation and swelling in tendinitis.
Analgesic: The analgesic effect of yarrow tincture, from compounds like salicylic acid, alleviates pain associated with tendinitis, improving comfort and mobility.
Antispasmodic: Yarrow tincture's antispasmodic property, owing to the presence of lactones, helps relax muscles and alleviate spasms in the affected area, reducing tenderness and discomfort.
Astringent: The astringent property of yarrow tincture, caused by tannins, helps constrict blood vessels and decrease fluid accumulation, promoting healing and reducing inflammation in tendinitis.

Circulation-boosting: Yarrow tincture's ability to improve circulation, due to the presence of volatile oils, aids in delivering nutrients and oxygen to the affected tendon, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate tendinitis-related pain and swelling.
Stems: Rich in therapeutic constituents, providing support for tendon health and tissue repair.
Leaves: High in flavonoids, offering relief from inflammation and promoting faster healing of tendons.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, helping to reduce oxidative stress and support tendon recovery.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids, providing nutritional support for tendon flexibility and overall joint health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh yarrow leaves and flowers, ensuring a minimum of one pound dry material for tincture production.
Chop the dried yarrow into small pieces to facilitate extraction, using scissors or mortar and pestle for approximately 10 minutes.
Combine the chopped yarrow with high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, utilizing a ratio of one part yarrow to five parts alcohol, and seal tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking occasionally, allowing the tincture to mature and extract essential compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tincture helps with tendinitis because it is known to have anti-inflammatory and circulatory properties, which can reduce swelling and promote healing in the affected tendons. The herb contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to help repair damaged tissue and reduce pain. Additionally, gotu kola has been used in traditional medicine to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, making it a popular natural remedy for tendinitis and other inflammatory conditions. Its ability to strengthen connective tissue and improve joint mobility also makes it an effective treatment for tendinitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Gotu kola tincture's ability to reduce inflammation is important for tendinitis as it helps alleviate pain and swelling in the affected tendon, with active constituents like asiaticoside and madecassoside contributing to this effect.

Improves circulation property: Gotu kola tincture promotes blood flow and microcirculation, which is crucial for tendinitis as it aids in delivering nutrients and oxygen to the injured tendon, facilitating healing, and reducing pain, thanks to triterpene saponins like brahmoside and brahminoside.

Promotes collagen synthesis property: Gotu kola tincture supports the production of collagen, an essential protein for tendon health and repair, which is vital for tendinitis as it helps rebuild and strengthen damaged tendons, with the pentacyclic triterpene acids (e.g., asiatic, madecassic, and maslinic acids) playing a significant role in this process.

Antioxidant property: Gotu kola tincture's antioxidant effects help protect tendon tissues from oxidative stress and damage, which is important for tendinitis as it reduces further degeneration and encourages healing, with the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids contributing to its antioxidant capacity.

Analgesic property: Gotu kola tincture's pain-relieving ability is crucial for tendinitis as it helps manage discomfort and improve mobility, with compounds like brahmin and isothankuniside contributing to its analgesic effect.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of gotu kola are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for tendinitis due to their anti-inflammatory and circulation-boosting properties.
Stems: Gotu kola stems are used in tinctures for tendinitis because they contain compounds that may help reduce pain and inflammation.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of gotu kola have been used in traditional medicine to treat inflammatory conditions, making them a valuable part of the plant for tendinitis tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gotu kola leaves from a clean environment, ensuring they are free of contaminants and pesticides.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove dirt and debris, then pat dry with a clean cloth.
Chop the clean gotu kola leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area for optimal extraction, using approximately 20 grams per batch.
Combine the chopped gotu kola with 80% ethanol or vodka in a 1:2 ratio by weight, steeping the mixture for 14-16 hours at room temperature to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the liquid tincture in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw tincture helps with tendinitis because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herb's active compounds, including harpagoside, have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation in the body, making it an effective natural remedy for tendinitis. Devil's claw tincture may help alleviate the swelling and discomfort associated with tendinitis, promoting healing and providing relief to individuals suffering from this condition. Its potent anti-inflammatory effects can help decrease the irritation and inflammation in the tendons, allowing for improved mobility and reduced pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Devil's claw tincture helps reduce inflammation in tendinitis due to the presence of iridoid glycosides like harpagoside, which inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals.
Analgesic: The herb's pain-relieving properties, attributed to harpagoside and other iridoid glycosides, help alleviate tendinitis-related pain by interfering with pain signaling in the body.

Improves mobility: Devil's claw tincture can help improve mobility and flexibility in tendons affected by tendinitis, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and muscle-relaxing properties, allowing for better movement and reduced stiffness.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of devil's claw, due to phenolic acids and flavonoids, aid in tendinitis recovery by scavenging free radicals and reducing oxidative stress in the affected area.

Immune system support: By supporting the immune system, devil's claw tincture can help the body better manage tendinitis symptoms, as a healthy immune system is better equipped to repair damaged tissues and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the devil's claw plant are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for tendinitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhizomes: Devil's claw rhizomes are rich in harpagosides, which are believed to help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation in tendinitis.
Seeds: The seeds of the devil's claw plant contain essential fatty acids and phytosterols, which may help reduce inflammation and support tendon healing.
Bark: The bark of the devil's claw plant contains iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making it useful for tendinitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried harpagophytum procumbens roots and chop them into small pieces weighing 1 kg per liter of solvent.
Combine the chopped root material with 70% ethanol in a clean glass container at a ratio of 1:3 w/v for 2 hours.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 2-4 weeks, shaking the container daily to ensure proper extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and preserving the liquid extract.
Store the resulting tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to 5 years, shaking well before use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat tendinitis with herbal tinctures is by using anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving herbs. For instance, a tincture made from turmeric or ginger can help reduce inflammation, while one made from valerian or skullcap can alleviate pain. Take the recommended dosage, usually 2-3 times a day, and combine with a topical herbal balm for optimal results. Always consult a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to tendinitis that are treated with herbal tinctures include bursitis and epicondylitis. Bursitis is the inflammation of the bursa, a small fluid-filled sac that cushions the joints, and is often treated with anti-inflammatory herbs such as willow bark and turmeric. Epicondylitis, also known as tennis elbow or golfer's elbow, is the inflammation of the tendons that attach to the bone on the outside or inside of the elbow, and can be treated with herbal tinctures containing arnica, St. John's wort, and calendula to reduce inflammation and promote healing. It is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment.
